title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 16th July 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: The worst is over; no need to sell at these levels.
recommendations.detail: A caller sitting on a 42% loss asked about averaging. Kutumba Rao said the worst is over for Tejas and told him to hold. The stock is consolidating now and this quarter's result is due. There is no need to sell at these levels.

recommendations.price: must cross 220-230 for a real move
recommendations.note: Underperformer; hold and see the result - needs to stand above 220-230.
recommendations.detail: A caller was at a 46% loss. Kutumba Rao said the result has to be seen because the stock has been such an underperformer - it showed a bit of a sizzle in between but weakness came straight back. For a real move it must cross and stand above the 220-230 levels. Last quarter's result was very disappointing and the stock fell as low as 150; if this result is better than expected, the stock could continue from here.

recommendations.note: No doubt at all - buy on every decline; water management theme is strong.
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said there is no doubt at all about VA Tech Wabag and one can buy it on every decline, because water treatment and water management companies will all do well from here. The El Nino effect showed just how important water management is. However, the stock has already risen a lot with a tremendous rally over the last year, so he said buy it only when good declines come.

recommendations.note: Wait for management confidence; too early to say the worst is over.
recommendations.detail: A caller thought Syngene looked attractively valued after the correction. Kutumba Rao said he would wait, because disappointment comes on and off in Syngene and it is still difficult to say the worst is over. This time not just the result but the management commentary will be key. He would wait for management confidence rather than enter the stock immediately - and that caution is reflected in the price, which is still floundering near its 52-week low.

recommendations.price: exit even on a technical rally (~Rs 60 now)
recommendations.note: Don't average a badly underperforming stock - exit; corporate governance issues.
recommendations.detail: A caller at a 25% loss asked about averaging. Kutumba Rao said no - one shouldn't keep averaging something that is badly underperforming, and Soma Distilleries underperforms a lot within the liquor space. He should have come out long ago: 'if you see one cockroach in the kitchen, understand there are ten more.' Since the management controversy and corporate governance issues a couple of years back, the stock has made continuous lower tops and lower bottoms down to Rs 60 today. Even if a technical rally comes or the price is operated better, it is better to exit.

recommendations.stock: United Spirits / Radico Khaitan / IFB Agro Industries
recommendations.note: Stick to the leader - the only three stocks worth owning in the liquor space.
recommendations.detail: Asked for an alternative in the liquor space after rejecting Soma Distilleries, Kutumba Rao said always stick to the leader. If you want to be in the liquor space there are only three stocks: United Spirits, Radico Khaitan and IFB Agro Industries. He named them as the leaders rather than giving individual entry levels.

recommendations.note: Avoidable stock - weak result with falling profits, as flagged many times.
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao noted he has said many times to avoid Sai Silks and Kala Mandir type companies. In line with that, the stock was weak in the market yesterday and the result showed profits falling. He called these avoidable stocks and said that should be remembered.

recommendations.note: Surprising weak fall on rumours; the time to observe the company has come.
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao flagged a surprising weak fall in Patanjali yesterday amid various rumours. With the weakness of the last three sessions the stock has now come below 350. He said one has to observe carefully what is happening in the company - the time to observe has come.

recommendations.note: Very good result with profit up ~40%; possible slow recovery.
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said he himself had thought HDB Financial Services was an underperformer, but the result was very good because profit rose almost 40%. On that basis he said maybe one can look at whether HDB Financial Services could stage a slow recovery.

recommendations.note: Don't buy at all - CERC norms will cut margins a lot next year.
recommendations.detail: A viewer wanted to enter IEX because the subsidiary Indian Gas Exchange IPO is coming. Kutumba Rao said that in fact if the stock rose at IPO time he was thinking of saying exit, while the viewer is thinking of buying. A short-term positional rally may come then, but the stock has become weak, and the CERC norms all start implementation in the coming year, after which margins will also fall a lot. It is not a great stock to hold as of now - avoid it, don't buy IEX at all.

recommendations.note: Don't average; the result is today - decide only after seeing it.
recommendations.detail: A viewer at a 25% loss asked whether to average or sell. Kutumba Rao said don't average. He pointed out the result is due today itself, so a decision can only be taken after seeing the result, and deferred the call to the next day's show.

recommendations.note: Certainly can buy; capacity expansion will show up in the numbers.
recommendations.detail: The same viewer was thinking of buying Valectra (name as heard in the captions). Kutumba Rao said certainly she can buy. Good expansion is happening in the company, and once the capacity expansion fully comes into availability, a lot of improvement will be visible in the numbers. Results are also due soon, in 10-15 days, so take a look then.

recommendations.note: Decent stock; trail a stop-loss if in profit - it's results season.
recommendations.detail: A viewer holding 20 shares of Azad Engineering at 1,350 asked whether to hold or sell. Kutumba Rao said they are all decent stocks and that he had bought decent stocks. He noted it wasn't clear whether the viewer is in profit or loss, but if in profit he would put a trailing stop-loss because it is results season.

recommendations.note: Decent stock; trail a stop-loss if in profit - it's results season.
recommendations.detail: Part of the same viewer's three-stock question. Kutumba Rao grouped Rossell Techsys with the others as a decent stock that was well bought. Without knowing whether the viewer is in profit or loss, his advice was to hold, and if in profit to put a trailing stop-loss given it is results season.

recommendations.note: Decent stock; trail a stop-loss if in profit - it's results season.
recommendations.detail: Part of the same viewer's three-stock question. Kutumba Rao said these are all decent stocks that were decently bought. His advice was to hold, and if the viewer is in profit, to put a trailing stop-loss because it is results season.

recommendations.note: Just wait - the NSE will be there; hold for now rather than buy fresh.
recommendations.detail: A viewer asked about buying BSE along with CDSL, SMS Pharma and Natco Pharma. On BSE, Kutumba Rao said one can just wait because the NSE will be there, and to hold BSE for now. He gave a clear preference for CDSL over a fresh BSE purchase.

recommendations.note: Start accumulating; duopoly sector and likely to outperform NSDL.
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said to start accumulating CDSL, calling it a good share for the long term. There are only two companies in this space for us - NSDL and CDSL - making it a duopoly sector. Between the two, he said the chances are visible that CDSL will outperform NSDL.

recommendations.note: Fully priced this month; wait for a significant correction, accumulate near 17,000.
recommendations.detail: Asked whether Force Motors can be taken at this level after its big rally and small correction, Kutumba Rao said this month he wouldn't advise it because the stock is fully priced. He would wait for a significant correction, though whether the correction comes to that extent can't be said. It has already come from around 25-26,000 to 18,380; maybe close to 17,000 one can start accumulating. There is no hurry with such a high-priced stock.

recommendations.note: Wait a quarter; July 15 record date already over - calculate the actual distribution yield.
recommendations.detail: A viewer holding 25,000 units bought at Rs 76 asked whether to hold or sell and when the dividend comes. Kutumba Rao said it is a hybrid instrument that listed only recently at what he thought was too high a price, and it has corrected continuously since to near its 52-week low - he too had thought that price wouldn't sustain. He would wait a quarter. The company had announced a record date of July 15 for the distribution, which is already over. In these InvITs the return on capital starts at just 1.7-2.1%; going by the details he was seeing it looked like about 1.12 per unit, roughly an 11-11.2% return, which is as good as a good dividend yield. He said to calculate the actual yield on the Rs 76 cost once the distribution comes and compare it year-on-year rather than answering off the cuff.
